basics.name: Andrew Westling
basics.label: Design Engineer
basics.summary: Design engineer with a formal background in architecture and a decade of experience in web development. Specialty in the intersection of design and engineering: building design systems, crafting resilient interfaces, and thinking holistically about how products are structured and scaled. Skilled with **TypeScript**, **React**, **Next.js**, **Tailwind**, agentic AI tooling, and more. Experienced doing platform-level work that multiplies the output of large engineering organizations.

work.name: Alloy
work.description: End-to-end identity and fraud prevention platform for companies that offer financial products
work.summary: Design engineering at an identity and fraud prevention platform for financial institutions. Solving problems where design and engineering overlap.
highlights: **Platform migration**: partnering with a small frontend platform team to lead an architectural migration of Alloy's frontend application, with rollout planned across ~50 product engineers
**Filter interfaces**: built new filter interfaces for Alloy's risk management platform, improving analyst workflow for complex, multi-dimensional data
**Design system tooling**: built an internal component usage reporting tool to analyze React component adoption across the codebase, enabling data-driven decisions about design system evolution
**Design system mentorship**: serve as the design system education resource for Alloy's product engineers, bridging design intent and implementation across teams

work.name: Droit (acquired by FIS)
work.description: Financial tools for decision-making and regulatory compliance
work.summary: Front-end development at a regulatory compliance platform for global financial institutions.
highlights: **Applications**: productionized and delivered the first version of a new application to help analysts validate transaction reports before they are sent to regulators
**Design systems**: improved build performance and developer experience of Droit's component library

work.name: Forte
work.summary: First engineering hire at a music education startup; built the application from scratch and set the technical and cultural foundation for a growing team.
highlights: **From scratch**: set up Forte's Next.js application from the ground up; chose tools and workflows to enable quick iteration; established a component library for consistent user experience

**Built features**: built the first iteration of various customer-facing features for Forte's marketplace: Teacher Profiles; Certified Institutions; Invitations and Sign Up; Teacher Ratings and Reviews; "Switch Role" functionality; etc.
**UX/Product Architecture**: informed the UX, product flow, and overall architecture of a multi-role marketplace application

work.name: Monograph
work.description: Practice management software for architecture firms
work.summary: Design system and feature development at a practice management platform for architecture firms.
highlights: **Design systems**: built components for Monograph's component library; wrote documentation and set up workflows between Figma and the codebase
**Features**: built customer-facing features: Team Directory; filter functionality for Monograph Resource; Settings UI; "Consultants" feature; etc.
**Glue work**: process documentation, tool selection, configuration, code reviews, data migration scripts, customer support collaboration, bug triaging; coordination that kept the team moving

work.name: Better.com
work.summary: Design system and feature development on Better's loan origination platform.
highlights: **Design systems**: established Better's Design Systems team; built libraries to empower engineering teams to build consistent and clean UIs

**Features**: built assorted borrower-facing features: Redisclosure Service; "Required Assets and Buffer" logic; Authentication/Session Expiration; UX for uploading a competitor's Loan Estimate; Loan Overview page; etc.
**Integrations**: extended integrations with sales and marketing tools (call scheduling, A/B testing, etc.)

work.name: Architizer
work.description: Marketplace for architects to find products for buildings
work.summary: Full-stack and front-end development at an architecture products marketplace.
highlights: **Component libraries**: established and maintained a UI component system to speed up feature development, and improve collaboration with designers
**Features**: built internal- and external-facing features: a private messaging interface; directory interfaces to Solr search indexes; etc.
**Integrations**: extended integrations with external tools for analytics, error logging, marketing, and customer support

work.name: EyeCue Lab
work.summary: Internship-to-hire at a product consultancy; built full-stack features and marketing pages across client projects.

work.name: Apple
work.summary: Technical support for macOS products while completing degree.
